canonical_definition: An explainability technique that aggregates attention weights across multiple layers of a model to create a comprehensive map of information flow from input to output tokens.
papers: Explaining Modern Gated-Linear RNNs via a Unified Implicit Attention Formulation
OATS: Outlier-Aware Pruning Through Sparse and Low Rank Decomposition

relationships.paper: OATS: Outlier-Aware Pruning Through Sparse and Low Rank Decomposition
relationships.evidence: “We explore the attention matrices with three explanation methods: raw attention, attention rollout (Abnar & Zuidema, 2020), and attribution”
